title: Residual pi-project-era 'project' naming debt across pi-context source/surface — package is pi-context + dir .context (DEC-0029) + surface /context, but source identifiers, files, Pi tools, and the command still say project
description: The 2026-05-08 rebuild renamed the package pi-project -> pi-context; DEC-0029 set the substrate dir to .context and named a forthcoming /context subcommand surface; FGAP-066 designs /context init onboarding. But the SOURCE still carries pi-project-era project naming throughout (~1079 occurrences in packages/pi-context/src): 6 project-*.ts files (project-sdk, project-context, project-dir + tests), 3 package.json subpath exports (./project-sdk, ./project-context, ./project-dir), ~13 Pi tool names + the /project command + customTypes (project-status, project-init, project-validate, project-validate-relations, project-edges-for-lens, project-walk-descendants, project-roadmap-load/render/validate/list, project-add-work, project-lens-curate), 12 exported project* identifiers (projectState, projectRoot, projectDir, validateProject, ...), and cross-package consumers in pi-jit-agents / pi-workflows / pi-behavior-monitors that import the ./project-* subpaths. This is INTERNAL-NAMING inconsistency debt, NOT a DEC-0025 vocabulary-neutrality violation: DEC-0025 governs the user-substrate CONCEPTION vocabulary (block kinds / relation types / statuses, derived from config), whereas project-sdk / projectState / /project are the framework own module + tool identifiers. The substrate-DIR name (.project via config.root) is config-data per DEC-0015 (resolveContextDir; the .project literal already removed from source per FGAP-035) and flips to .context at cutover (DEC-0029) — a separate concern from this source-identifier debt. Intended outcome: rename the project->context source identifier surface (project-sdk.ts -> context-sdk.ts; project-context.ts / project-dir.ts -> context equivalents; projectState/projectRoot/projectDir/validateProject -> context or substrate equivalents; /project -> /context command; project-* -> context-* Pi tools; ./project-* -> ./context-* subpath exports; + every cross-package consumer import + tests) so code matches the package / dir / surface name. OPEN sub-decision (settle first, worth a DEC): the target-naming scheme — e.g. project-context.ts is itself awkward (context-context); candidates context-core / context-substrate; projectRoot/projectDir -> contextRoot/contextDir; validateProject -> validateContext or validateSubstrate. Mechanism: a source-wide codemod across all four packages + tool/command registrations + package.json subpaths + consumer imports + tests — DISTINCT from the FGAP-060 renameCanonicalId engine (which renames SUBSTRATE canonical_ids, not source code). Until this FGAP it was captured only as HANDOFF re-derivation step-4 prose + the plan file (a DEC-0040 single-source violation this filing corrects). [ORGANIZING POINTER 2026-05-22] The substrate-dir-name concern this gap scopes out as a separate concern is now filed as FGAP-094 (shipped .project default in samples/conception.json). Residual-project cluster = FGAP-074 (source/surface identifier naming) + FGAP-094 (shipped substrate-dir default) + DEC-0036 step-4 (/project -> /context command rename, within 074 scope) + DEC-0036 step-5 (.project -> .context dir cutover).

evidence.reference: SDK named project-sdk despite pi-context package / .context dir / /context surface; exports projectState/validateProject/buildIdIndex etc.
./project-sdk, ./project-context, ./project-dir subpath exports (consumed cross-package) carry the old name
~13 project-* Pi tool names + /project command + customTypes

DEC-0029 (.context dir + forthcoming /context surface) — code lags it; DEC-0025 (vocabulary-neutral) is a DISTINCT concern (user vocab, not framework identifiers); DEC-0040 (single-source) — this rename was prose-only until now

impact: Code/surface name mismatch (pi-context package + .context dir + /context surface vs project-* source) confuses adopters and every fresh LLM, and the /context surface (FGAP-026 Phase 6 + FGAP-066 onboarding) cannot land cleanly without it. Was untracked in substrate (HANDOFF/plan prose only) — a DEC-0040 violation.
proposed_resolution: Settle the target-naming scheme (open sub-decision; candidate a DEC), then codemod the project->context source identifier surface (files / exports / Pi tools / command / package subpaths / cross-package consumers / tests) to match package + dir + surface. Outcome-only; not the FGAP-060 substrate canonical_id engine (this is source code). Sequenced as re-derivation step 4 (extension refactor), after step 3 re-populate + before/with cutover (step 5) — exact sequencing the user's call.
